$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocomplete/validation; ignored at load time

name.description: Display name of the plugin author or organization

email.description: Contact email for support or feedback

url.description: Website, GitHub profile, or organization URL

plugins.description: Collection of available plugins in this marketplace

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocomplete/validation; ignored at load time

name.description: Unique identifier matching the plugin name

description.description: Brief, user-facing explanation of what the plugin provides

author.description: Information about the plugin creator or maintainer

name.description: Display name of the plugin author or organization

email.description: Contact email for support or feedback

url.description: Website, GitHub profile, or organization URL

keywords.description: Tags for plugin discovery and categorization

dependencies.description: Plugins that must be enabled for this plugin to function. Bare names (no "@marketplace") are resolved against the declaring plugin's own marketplace.

anyOf.description: Path to file with additional hooks (in addition to those in hooks/hooks.json, if it exists), relative to the plugin root
Additional hooks (in addition to those in hooks/hooks.json, if it exists)

matcher.description: String pattern to match (e.g. tool names like "Write")

hooks.description: List of hooks to execute when the matcher matches

if.description: Permission rule syntax to filter when this hook runs (e.g., "Bash(git *)"). Only runs if the tool call matches the pattern. Avoids spawning hooks for non-matching commands.

shell.description: Shell interpreter. 'bash' uses your $SHELL (bash/zsh/sh); 'powershell' uses pwsh. Defaults to bash.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for this specific command

statusMessage.description: Custom status message to display in spinner while hook runs

once.description: If true, hook runs once and is removed after execution

async.description: If true, hook runs in background without blocking

asyncRewake.description: If true, hook runs in background and wakes the model on exit code 2 (blocking error). Implies async.

prompt.description: Prompt to evaluate with LLM. Use $ARGUMENTS placeholder for hook input JSON.

if.description: Permission rule syntax to filter when this hook runs (e.g., "Bash(git *)"). Only runs if the tool call matches the pattern. Avoids spawning hooks for non-matching commands.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for this specific prompt evaluation

model.description: Model to use for this prompt hook (e.g., "claude-sonnet-4-6"). If not specified, uses the default small fast model.

statusMessage.description: Custom status message to display in spinner while hook runs

once.description: If true, hook runs once and is removed after execution

prompt.description: Prompt describing what to verify (e.g. "Verify that unit tests ran and passed."). Use $ARGUMENTS placeholder for hook input JSON.

if.description: Permission rule syntax to filter when this hook runs (e.g., "Bash(git *)"). Only runs if the tool call matches the pattern. Avoids spawning hooks for non-matching commands.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for agent execution (default 60)

model.description: Model to use for this agent hook (e.g., "claude-sonnet-4-6"). If not specified, uses Haiku.

statusMessage.description: Custom status message to display in spinner while hook runs

once.description: If true, hook runs once and is removed after execution

url.description: URL to POST the hook input JSON to

if.description: Permission rule syntax to filter when this hook runs (e.g., "Bash(git *)"). Only runs if the tool call matches the pattern. Avoids spawning hooks for non-matching commands.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for this specific request

headers.description: Additional headers to include in the request. Values may reference environment variables using $VAR_NAME or ${VAR_NAME} syntax (e.g., "Authorization": "Bearer $MY_TOKEN"). Only variables listed in allowedEnvVars will be interpolated.

allowedEnvVars.description: Explicit list of environment variable names that may be interpolated in header values. Only variables listed here will be resolved; all other $VAR references are left as empty strings. Required for env var interpolation to work.

statusMessage.description: Custom status message to display in spinner while hook runs

once.description: If true, hook runs once and is removed after execution

server.description: Name of an already-configured MCP server to invoke

tool.description: Name of the tool on that server to call

input.description: Arguments passed to the MCP tool. String values support ${path} interpolation from the hook input JSON (e.g. "${tool_input.file_path}").

if.description: Permission rule syntax to filter when this hook runs (e.g., "Bash(git *)"). Only runs if the tool call matches the pattern. Avoids spawning hooks for non-matching commands.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for this specific tool call

statusMessage.description: Custom status message to display in spinner while hook runs

once.description: If true, hook runs once and is removed after execution

anyOf.description: Path to additional command file or skill directory (in addition to those in the commands/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root
List of paths to additional command files or skill directories

items.description: Path to additional command file or skill directory (in addition to those in the commands/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root

anyOf.description: Object mapping of command names to their metadata and source files. Command name becomes the slash command name (e.g., "about" → "/plugin:about")

source.description: Path to command markdown file, relative to plugin root

content.description: Inline markdown content for the command

anyOf.description: Path to additional agent file (in addition to those in the agents/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root
List of paths to additional agent files

items.description: Path to additional agent file (in addition to those in the agents/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root

anyOf.description: Path to additional skill directory (in addition to those in the skills/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root
List of paths to additional skill directories

items.description: Path to additional skill directory (in addition to those in the skills/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root

anyOf.description: Path to additional output styles directory or file (in addition to those in the output-styles/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root
List of paths to additional output styles directories or files

items.description: Path to additional output styles directory or file (in addition to those in the output-styles/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root

anyOf.description: Path to additional themes directory or file (in addition to those in the themes/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root
List of paths to additional themes directories or files

items.description: Path to additional themes directory or file (in addition to those in the themes/ directory, if it exists), relative to the plugin root

channels.description: Channels this plugin provides. Each entry declares an MCP server as a message channel and optionally specifies user configuration to prompt for at enable time.

server.description: Name of the MCP server this channel binds to. Must match a key in this plugin's mcpServers.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the config dialog title (e.g., "Telegram"). Defaults to the server name.

userConfig.description: Fields to prompt the user for when enabling this plugin in assistant mode. Saved values are substituted into ${user_config.KEY} references in the mcpServers env.

title.description: Human-readable label shown in the config dialog

description.description: Help text shown beneath the field in the config dialog

required.description: If true, validation fails when this field is empty

default.description: Default value used when the user provides nothing

multiple.description: For string type: allow an array of strings

sensitive.description: If true, masks dialog input and stores value in secure storage (keychain/credentials file) instead of settings.json

anyOf.description: MCP servers to include in the plugin (in addition to those in the .mcp.json file, if it exists)
Path or URL to MCPB file containing MCP server configuration
Path to MCPB file relative to plugin root
URL to MCPB file
MCP server configurations keyed by server name
Array of MCP server configurations (paths, MCPB files, or inline definitions)
Path to MCP servers configuration file
Path or URL to MCPB file
Path to MCPB file relative to plugin root
URL to MCPB file
Inline MCP server configurations
Path to .lsp.json configuration file relative to plugin root
LSP server configurations keyed by server name

command.description: Command to execute the LSP server (e.g., "typescript-language-server")

args.description: Command-line arguments to pass to the server

extensionToLanguage.description: Mapping from file extension to LSP language ID. File extensions and languages are derived from this mapping.

env.description: Environment variables to set when starting the server

initializationOptions.description: Initialization options passed to the server during initialization

settings.description: Settings passed to the server via workspace/didChangeConfiguration

workspaceFolder.description: Workspace folder path to use for the server

startupTimeout.description: Maximum time to wait for server startup (milliseconds)

shutdownTimeout.description: Maximum time to wait for graceful shutdown (milliseconds)

restartOnCrash.description: Whether to restart the server if it crashes

maxRestarts.description: Maximum number of restart attempts before giving up

anyOf.description: Array of LSP server configurations (paths or inline definitions)
Path to LSP configuration file
Inline LSP server configurations

command.description: Command to execute the LSP server (e.g., "typescript-language-server")

args.description: Command-line arguments to pass to the server

extensionToLanguage.description: Mapping from file extension to LSP language ID. File extensions and languages are derived from this mapping.

env.description: Environment variables to set when starting the server

initializationOptions.description: Initialization options passed to the server during initialization

settings.description: Settings passed to the server via workspace/didChangeConfiguration

workspaceFolder.description: Workspace folder path to use for the server

startupTimeout.description: Maximum time to wait for server startup (milliseconds)

shutdownTimeout.description: Maximum time to wait for graceful shutdown (milliseconds)

restartOnCrash.description: Whether to restart the server if it crashes

maxRestarts.description: Maximum number of restart attempts before giving up

monitors.description: Background watch scripts the host arms as persistent Monitor tasks (unsandboxed, same trust tier as hooks) so plugins need not instruct the model to arm them. When omitted, monitors/monitors.json at the plugin root is loaded if present.

anyOf.description: Path to a JSON file containing the monitors array, relative to the plugin root

name.description: Identifier for this monitor, unique within the plugin. Used to dedupe so re-arming (plugin reload, repeat skill invoke) does not spawn duplicates.

command.description: Shell command to run as a persistent background monitor. Each stdout line is delivered to the model as a <task_notification> event; the process runs for the session lifetime. ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}, ${user_config.*}, and ${ENV_VAR} are substituted. Runs in the session cwd — prefix with `cd "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}" && ` if the script needs its own directory.

description.description: Short human-readable description of what is being monitored (shown in task panel and notification summary).

when.description: Arm trigger. "always" arms at session start and on plugin reload. "on-skill-invoke:<skill>" arms the first time that skill is dispatched (via Skill tool or slash command).

settings.description: Settings to merge into the user settings while this plugin is enabled. Only the documented allowlisted keys are applied.

userConfig.description: User-configurable values this plugin needs. Prompted at enable time. Non-sensitive values saved to settings.json; sensitive values to secure storage. Available as ${user_config.KEY} in MCP/LSP server config, hook commands, and (non-sensitive only) skill/agent content. Keep sensitive value counts small.

title.description: Human-readable label shown in the config dialog

description.description: Help text shown beneath the field in the config dialog

required.description: If true, validation fails when this field is empty

default.description: Default value used when the user provides nothing

multiple.description: For string type: allow an array of strings

sensitive.description: If true, masks dialog input and stores value in secure storage (keychain/credentials file) instead of settings.json

source.description: Where to fetch the plugin from

anyOf.description: Path to the plugin root, relative to the marketplace root (the directory containing .claude-plugin/, not .claude-plugin/ itself)
NPM package as plugin source

package.description: Package name (or url, or local path, or anything else that can be passed to `npm` as a package)

registry.description: Custom NPM registry URL (defaults to using system default, likely npmjs.org)

url.description: Full git repository URL (https:// or git@)

ref.description: Git branch or tag to use (e.g., "main", "v1.0.0"). Defaults to repository default branch.
Git branch or tag to use (e.g., "main", "v1.0.0"). Defaults to repository default branch.

anyOf.description: Plugin located in a subdirectory of a larger repository (monorepo). Only the specified subdirectory is materialized; the rest of the repo is not downloaded.

url.description: Git repository: GitHub owner/repo shorthand, https://, or git@ URL

path.description: Subdirectory within the repo containing the plugin (e.g., "tools/claude-plugin"). Cloned sparsely using partial clone (--filter=tree:0) to minimize bandwidth for monorepos.

ref.description: Git branch or tag to use (e.g., "main", "v1.0.0"). Defaults to repository default branch.

strict.description: Require the plugin manifest to be present in the plugin folder. If false, the marketplace entry provides the manifest.

forceRemoveDeletedPlugins.description: When true, plugins removed from this marketplace will be automatically uninstalled and flagged for users

pluginRoot.description: Base path for relative plugin sources

allowCrossMarketplaceDependenciesOn.description: Marketplace names whose plugins may be auto-installed as dependencies. Only the root marketplace's allowlist applies — no transitive trust.

title: Claude Code Plugin Marketplace
description: Marketplace manifest (.claude-plugin/marketplace.json) listing Claude Code plugins. Learn more: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces
